data.title: Bitcoin crashes below $85K as global markets reel in widespread selloff
data.description:
Bitcoin just broke below $85,000 for the first time since March, after a straight-line 45-day collapse triggered by forced liquidations and tariff-driven macro shocks. Next support is unclear, with some traders eyeing the $77K–$80K zone. Equities just had their worst session since June, with the S&P 500 losing $2 trillion in value in under six […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/bitcoin-crashes-below-85k/

data.title: Tom Lee: The recent sell-off is partly due to market makers who suffered losses in the 1011 incident.
data.description: In an interview with CNBC, BitMine Chairman Tom Lee stated that the recent sell-off partly stemmed from market makers affected by the 1011 incident, and not just one. These market makers have deficits on their balance sheets and need to replenish funds, thus shrinking their balance sheets, reducing trading, and selling tokens. If the prices of mainstream tokens continue to fall, they will need to sell even more tokens to ensure they have cash on hand.

data.title: Asia Market Open: Bitcoin Tumbles 7% to $85K, Stocks Retreat as US Jobs Data Fails to Clarify Rate Outlook
data.description:
Bitcoin slid 7% to $85,000 as Asia opened lower, with traders dumping risk assets after mixed US jobs data and rising macro anxiety.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/bitcoin-price-85k-stocks-slide-jobs-data-clouds-rate-outlook-asia-market-open/

data.title: MSCI index likely to kick out crypto treasuries, exec warns
data.description:

data.title: A whale deposited 2.24 million USDC into Hyperliquid and shorted BTC with 40x leverage.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale deposited 2.24 million USDC into Hyperliquid and opened a short position in Bitcoin with 40x leverage.

data.title: Mike Alfred: Someone is trying to drive down the price of Bitcoin and scare investors into selling through derivatives.
data.description: Mike Alfred, founder of Alpine Fox LP, wrote, "Institutions are going to great lengths to manipulate derivatives such as perpetual contracts and futures in an attempt to depress the price of Bitcoin and scare investors into selling. This is one of the biggest scams in the market." Tom Lee, chairman of BitMine, retweeted and expressed his agreement.

data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description: 1. Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net outflow of $903 million yesterday, the second highest in history; 2. Ethereum spot ETFs saw a net outflow of $262 million yesterday, marking the eighth consecutive day of net outflows; 3. Viewpoint: BTC is likely to find support at $81,800 or $74,800, with $74,800 potentially being the bottom of this bear market; 4. Viewpoint: The recent decline in the crypto market was mainly driven by retail investors selling off Bitcoin and Ethereum spot ETFs; 5. The total market capitalization of cryptocurrencies fell to $3.042 trillion, a 5.5% decrease in the last 24 hours; 6. Tom Lee: The deleveraging cycle in the crypto market may be nearing its end; 7. Ark Invest increased its holdings in Circle, BitMine, and Robinhood yesterday; 8. The whale that previously borrowed 66,000 ETH to short sell has again increased its holdings of ETH by over $162 million, bringing its total holdings to 432,718 ETH. ETH; 9. "Short selling profits of $24.48 million" A whale withdrew 57,700 ETH from Binance this morning.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $903 million yesterday, the second highest in history.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $903 million yesterday (November 20th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF (IBIT) experienced the largest single-day net outflow at $355 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $62.826 billion. This was followed by the Grayscale ETF (GBTC), with a net outflow of $199 million, bringing its total historical net outflow to $25.095 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs was $113.02 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.55%, and a cumulative historical net inflow of $57.397 billion.

data.title: Nakamoto's CEO clarified that the 367 bitcoins were used for DAT investment, not as a true "sale."
data.description: In response to market disclosures that Nakamoto, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin treasury company, had sold 367 bitcoins, CEO David Bailey clarified on the X platform that these bitcoins were used for equity investments in digital asset treasury companies (DATs), including Metaplanet, Treasury, and Future. Therefore, it was not a true "sale," but rather the bitcoin balance on the company's balance sheet did not reflect the value of these equity investments.

data.title: Bitcoin falls to 9th place in global asset market capitalization rankings
data.description: According to the latest data from 8marketcap, Bitcoin has been surpassed by the Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF, falling to ninth place in global asset market capitalization rankings. Data shows that Bitcoin's current market capitalization has dropped to $1.703 trillion, a 24-hour decrease of 7.47% and a 7-day decrease of 13.12%.

data.title: Deutsche Bank goes bullish on Bullish post-earnings beat
data.description:
Deutsche Bank just flipped the switch on Bullish. After months of holding back, the bank is now telling investors to buy the crypto exchange’s stock. The call came straight after Bullish posted its third-quarter results, which showed stronger-than-expected revenue, even though profits didn’t meet estimates. This new buy rating came with a slightly lower target: […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/deutsche-bank-goes-bullish-on-bullish/

data.title: JPMorgan Revises Forecast on Federal Reserve's December Rate Cut
data.description: According to BlockBeats, JPMorgan has updated its forecast regarding the Federal Reserve's monetary policy, no longer anticipating a rate cut in December. Previously, the financial institution had predicted a 25 basis point reduction. This change reflects a shift in expectations for the central bank's approach to interest rates.

data.title: Grok fan-girling Elon Musk shows why AI must be decentralized
data.description:
